title: Spinach Butternut Squash Salad
ingredients: 1 1/4 lbs butternut squash, peeled and diced 3/4-inch
kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
5 oz baby spinach, washed and spun dry
instructions: In a large bowl, toss the butternut squash with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, 1 tablespoon honey, salt and fresh ground pepper.
Place the squash mixture on a baking sheet and roast in the center of the oven for 20-25 minutes, turning halfway through, or until tender.
Remove the roasted squash from the oven and let it cool to room temperature.
Meanwhile, combine the vinegar, shallots, remaining honey, and mustard in a bowl.
Whisk in the oil, a pinch of salt, and black pepper to make the vinaigrette.
Top each salad with the roasted butternut squash, pumpkin seeds, dried cherries, and crumbled gorgonzola cheese.
Drizzle the dressing over each salad and serve immediately.
notes: Make sure to wash and spin dry the spinach before using to remove excess moisture
The roasted squash can be prepared ahead and stored in the refrigerator until ready to assemble the salad
For a variation, substitute the gorgonzola with feta or goat cheese if preferred
